context: Her last great word to the movement.

text: It was we, the people; not we, the white male citizens; nor yet we, the male citizens; but we, the whole people, who formed the Union.

context: Naming the vote as the hinge of citizenship.

text: Cautious, careful people, always casting about to preserve their reputation and social standing, never can bring about a reform.
Men, their rights, and nothing more; women, their rights, and nothing less.
context: Program of equal rights without excess or deficit.

text: I declare to you that woman must not depend upon the protection of man, but must be taught to protect herself.
context: Rejecting paternal guardianship as substitute for rights.

text: There never will be complete equality until women themselves help to make laws and elect lawmakers.
